Each non-background object in `b745798f` is a 3-cell `2x2` corner marker; its orientation, not its location, determines which outer corner it expands to. The verifier redraws each marker as a large L of the same color anchored at that grid corner, stopping before the central row and column.

The summary hint was used as a starting hypothesis and confirmed against the official examples, so no `notes.md` was needed. The generator matches the task family by building odd square `8`-background grids with exactly four corner-marker objects, `3` or `4` active colors, occasional side-pair color reuse, and sparse placement similar to the official inputs. No new primitive was needed in `arc2/dsl_ext.py`.

Official verification passed `4/4`, generated verification passed `1000/1000`, and the preview sheet looked on-family.
